Regulation_Genes.Variety: B73; B73 × Mo17 F1 hybrid seedlings used for protoplast assays
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant environmental interaction; structural and protective specialized metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Binds promoters of phenolic pathway genes including HCT6 and COMT1; implicated in regulation of phenolic metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: HCT6; COMT1; 4CL3
Regulation_Genes.Direct_or_Indirect_Regulator: Direct for HCT6 and COMT1 by Y1H and ChIP; possible direct or indirect association with 4CL3 by ChIP only
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Multi-target regulator linked to phenolic pathway gene promoters
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Maize leaf protoplast transient transformation; ChIP-qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB100 was identified by Y1H as binding phenolic gene promoters including HCT6 and COMT1. 2. ChIP-qPCR in maize protoplasts confirmed in vivo association with HCT6 and COMT1. 3. ChIP also detected association with 4CL3, which was not recovered in Y1H, highlighting complementary detection by Y1H and ChIP.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential regulator for engineering phenolic pathway output
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: CAGE used to define some promoter TSSs; no accession reported. Existing ChIP-seq data integrated for other TFs only; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as an example for concordance and discrepancy between Y1H and ChIP results

Regulation_Genes.Variety: B73; B73 × Mo17 F1 hybrid seedlings used for protoplast assays
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB family; ortholog of Arabidopsis AtMYB58/AtMYB63
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant environmental interaction; structural and protective specialized metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a transcriptional activator of phenylpropanoid genes and likely promotes phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PAL1; PAL3; PAL4; PAL5; PAL6; PAL8; PAL9; and other phenolic gene promoters totaling 13
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PAL4 reporter; likely activates additional bound phenolic genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: High-connectivity positive regulator with validated promoter binding and transactivation activity
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Maize leaf protoplast transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; ChIP-qPCR; transient transactivation assay using pPAL4::Luc
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB65 recognized 13 phenolic gene regulatory sequences, including seven PAL promoters. 2. ChIP-qPCR confirmed 10 of 13 Y1H interactions in maize protoplasts. 3. p35S::MYB65-GFP strongly activated pPAL4::Luc, demonstrating activator function in maize.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Promising target for manipulating phenylpropanoid flux and lignin/related phenolics
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: CAGE used to define some promoter TSSs; no accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented as maize ortholog of Arabidopsis lignin-associated activators AtMYB58 and AtMYB63

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant environmental interaction; structural and protective specialized metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Recognizes 24 gene promoters spanning phenylpropanoid and flavonoid pathways, suggesting broad coordination of phenolic biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 24 phenolic gene promoters across pathway branches
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Highest-connectivity TF in the network portion analyzed
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Y1H assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LBD35 recognized 24 phenolic gene promoters. 2. It bound promoters in both phenylpropanoid and flavonoid pathways. 3. Its broad connectivity suggests a coordinating role in pathway gene expression.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Candidate broad-spectrum regulator for phenolic pathway engineering
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Ortholog function unknown; pleiotropy possible due to LBD roles in development
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functions of LBD35 or orthologs in other plants remain unknown

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: LBD/LOB domain family; ortholog of Arabidopsis LBD16
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant environmental interaction; structural and protective specialized metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Recognizes 23 phenolic gene promoters across pathway branches, suggesting broad regulation of phenolic metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 23 phenolic gene promoters across phenylpropanoid and flavonoid pathways
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: One of the top high-connectivity regulators in the network
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Y1H assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LBD24 recognized 23 phenolic promoters. 2. It is among the most highly connected regulators in the phenolic GRN. 3. Its Arabidopsis ortholog acts as a transcriptional activator in lateral root formation, but its phenolic role in maize is newly suggested here.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Candidate regulator for coordinated alteration of multiple phenolic branches
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible developmental pleiotropy based on LBD family functions
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AtLBD16 corresponds to the ortholog of LBD24

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant environmental interaction; structural and protective specialized metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Recognizes 18 phenolic gene promoters across pathway branches
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 18 phenolic gene promoters across phenylpropanoid and flavonoid pathways
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: High-connectivity regulator potentially coordinating pathway expression
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Y1H assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LBD20 bound 18 phenolic gene promoters. 2. It belongs to the subset of regulators recognizing 10 or more promoters. 3. Its broad promoter binding suggests a coordinating role in phenolic metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential multi-gene engineering target for phenolic traits
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functions of LBD20 or orthologs in other plants remain unknown

Regulation_Genes.Variety: B73; B73 × Mo17 F1 hybrid seedlings used for protoplast assays
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant environmental interaction; structural and protective specialized metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Recognizes 15 phenolic gene promoters across all major branches
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: HCT10; CCR3; 4CL3; C2; plus additional promoters totaling 15
Regulation_Genes.Direct_or_Indirect_Regulator: Direct for HCT10 by Y1H and ChIP-supported association; some targets detected only by ChIP
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Broadly connected regulator spanning phenylpropanoid, lignin, and flavonoid branches
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Y1H and ChIP-qPCR in maize protoplasts
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DOF28 recognized 15 phenolic gene promoters from the phenylpropanoid, lignin, and flavonoid branches. 2. ChIP confirmed HCT10 association and detected additional associations such as 4CL3 and C2 not seen in Y1H. 3. DOF28 is one of the 11 high-connectivity TFs in the network.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential regulator for coordinated modification of multiple phenolic outputs
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Compared with Arabidopsis AtDOF4;2, which represses flavonoid and activates lignin genes

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant environmental interaction; structural and protective specialized metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Binds 13 promoters across all branches of the phenolic pathway
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 13 phenolic gene promoters across all branches
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Broad multi-branch regulator from an undercharacterized TF family
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Y1H assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPP8 was identified as binding 13 phenolic gene promoters. 2. Its targets span all branches of the phenolic pathway. 3. This suggests a previously unrecognized role of CPP TFs in maize phenolic metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Novel candidate regulator for pathway engineering
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CPP family is poorly characterized in plants; this study expands its functional scope

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant environmental interaction; structural and protective specialized metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Recognizes 13 promoters across all branches of phenolic biosynthesis, linking nitrogen status regulators to phenolic metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 13 phenolic gene promoters across all branches
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Suggests cross-talk between nitrogen signaling and phenolic pathway control
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Nitrogen cellular status; primary nitrate response context inferred from NLP family function
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Y1H assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NLP9 recognized 13 promoters corresponding to all branches of phenolic biosynthesis. 2. The result suggests a previously unrecognized link between phenolic biosynthesis and nitrogen cellular status. 3. NLP9 is among the most connected regulators identified.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential node for integrating nutrient status with phenolic quality traits
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper text inconsistently lists NLP14 among top TFs but describes NLP9 as recognizing 13 promoters; extraction follows detailed Results text for NLP9

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant environmental interaction; structural and protective specialized metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Recognizes regulatory regions of 12 genes across all branches of the phenolic pathway
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 12 phenolic gene promoters across all branches
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Highly connected regulator in the phenolic GRN
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Y1H assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bZIP63 recognized 12 phenolic gene regulatory regions. 2. Its targets span all branches of the phenolic pathway. 3. It is one of the 11 TFs recognizing 10 or more promoters.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Candidate regulator for broad phenolic trait modulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper initially mentions bZIP13 among top TFs, but the detailed Results text specifies bZIP63 with 12 targets; extraction follows detailed Results text

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant environmental interaction; structural and protective specialized metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Binds promoters of 10 phenolic pathway genes, especially lignin-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PAL9; 4CL3; ALDH1; ALDH5; COMT1; F5H1; HCT6; HCT10; FLS1; C2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Connects general phenylpropanoid, lignin, and flavonoid branches through multi-promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Y1H assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C3H42 was shown to bind 10 phenolic promoters. 2. Targets include two general phenylpropanoid genes, six lignin-associated genes, and two flavonoid genes. 3. This positions C3H42 as a multi-branch phenolic regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential leverage point for modifying lignin and associated phenolic traits
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper text says eight lignin genes but lists six names; extraction uses explicitly listed targets

Regulation_Genes.Variety: B73; B73 × Mo17 F1 hybrid seedlings used for protoplast assays
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant environmental interaction; structural and protective specialized metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Recognizes 10 phenolic biosynthesis promoters and associates in vivo with Bz2 and PAL5 promoters
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Bz2; PAL5; plus additional promoters totaling 10
Regulation_Genes.Direct_or_Indirect_Regulator: Direct promoter recognition in Y1H; in vivo association supported by ChIP
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Co-regulator family member with direct promoter recognition, extending known MBF1 functions to phenolic metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TBP implied for MBF1 family generally; specific interactors in this study NA
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Maize protoplast transient ChIP assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MBF1.3 recognized 10 phenolic biosynthesis promoters in Y1H. 2. ChIP-qPCR validated in vivo interactions with Bz2 and PAL5. 3. The study provides evidence that an MBF1 family member directly participates in phenolic gene regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential co-regulator target for tuning phenolic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors discuss MBF1 as a family blurring TF/co-regulator distinction

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant environmental interaction; structural and protective specialized metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Identified among TFs recognizing 10 or more phenolic gene promoters, suggesting broad regulatory role
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 10 or more phenolic gene promoters
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: High-connectivity MYB regulator in the phenolic GRN
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Y1H assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB19 belongs to the subset of TFs recognizing 10 or more phenolic promoters. 2. Its broad binding suggests a coordinating role in pathway regulation. 3. No direct functional validation beyond Y1H is described in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Candidate regulator for broad phenolic pathway manipulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific promoter count not detailed in the main text beyond inclusion among top binders

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: NA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cooperates with EREB12 to activate Bz1 promoter; alone insufficient in yeast selection assay
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Bz1
Regulation_Genes.Direct_or_Indirect_Regulator: Direct or cooperative direct promoter regulation unresolved
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Required together with EREB12 for Bz1 promoter activation in yeast
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Yeast one-hybrid selection medium with 40 mM 3-AT; Y2H assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-transformation Y1H cooperation assay; Y2H physical interaction assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZHD14 and EREB12 were recovered together from a yeast strain activating the Bz1 promoter. 2. Separate transformations failed to activate Bz1, whereas co-expression enabled growth, indicating cooperation. 3. Y2H showed physical interaction between ZHD14 and EREB12.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Highlights combinatorial regulation of anthocyanin genes as engineering consideration
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents one of four cooperative TF pairs with demonstrated physical interaction

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: NA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cooperates with ZHD14 to activate Bz1 promoter; alone insufficient in yeast selection assay
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Bz1
Regulation_Genes.Direct_or_Indirect_Regulator: Direct or cooperative direct promoter regulation unresolved
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Required together with ZHD14 for Bz1 promoter activation in yeast
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Yeast one-hybrid selection medium with 40 mM 3-AT; Y2H assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-transformation Y1H cooperation assay; Y2H physical interaction assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EREB12 and ZHD14 were jointly required to activate the Bz1 promoter in yeast. 2. Neither factor alone supported activation under selective conditions. 3. EREB12 physically interacted with ZHD14 in Y2H.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Suggests combinatorial control points in anthocyanin engineering
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents cooperative TF regulation rather than single-factor activation

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: NA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Works together with NAC37 to activate Bz1 promoter, but alone activates A1 better and NAC37 impairs A1 activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: combinatorial transcriptional activation with promoter-dependent modulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Bz1; A1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates Bz1 with NAC37; activates A1 alone; NAC37 impairs A1 activation by LBD4
Regulation_Genes.Direct_or_Indirect_Regulator: Direct or cooperative direct promoter regulation unresolved
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Promoter-specific regulator of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes with context-dependent combinatorial behavior
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Yeast one-hybrid selection medium; Y2H assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-transformation Y1H cooperation assay; Y2H physical interaction assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LBD4 and NAC37 were together required for activation of the Bz1 promoter in yeast. 2. LBD4 alone was sufficient to activate A1. 3. NAC37 reduced LBD4-mediated activation on A1, and the two proteins physically interacted in Y2H.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Important for engineering anthocyanin genes where promoter context and TF combinations matter
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential promoter-specific antagonism when combined with NAC37
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Clear example of combinatorial and promoter-specific regulation

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation and protective specialized metabolite functions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required with LBD4 for Bz1 activation and modulates LBD4 activity on A1 negatively in yeast
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Bz1; A1 (through LBD4-dependent modulation)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Co-activates Bz1 with LBD4; impairs LBD4 activation of A1
Regulation_Genes.Direct_or_Indirect_Regulator: Direct or cooperative direct promoter regulation unresolved
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Acts as a combinatorial partner that changes promoter-specific output of anthocyanin genes
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Yeast one-hybrid selection medium; Y2H assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-transformation Y1H cooperation assay; Y2H physical interaction assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NAC37 cooperated with LBD4 to activate the Bz1 promoter. 2. On the A1 promoter, NAC37 impaired the activation mediated by LBD4. 3. NAC37 physically interacted with LBD4 in Y2H.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Shows that anthocyanin engineering may require controlling TF combinations rather than single genes
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promoter-specific negative modulation of partner TF activity
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function inferred from yeast combinatorial assays only

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant environmental interaction; structural and protective specialized metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Participates in a cooperative TF pair with MYB73 and physically interacts in Y2H
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: NA
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Evidence supports pairwise cooperative role in phenolic gene regulation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Y2H assay following identification as cooperative pair in Y1H
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB70 was part of a TF pair identified as needing to work together for transcriptional activation in yeast. 2. MYB70 physically interacted with MYB73 in Y2H. 3. Specific target promoter details were not provided in the main text.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Indicates possible MYB-MYB combinatorial control points in phenolic regulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Main-text target promoter not specified; additional details likely in supplemental data

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Indicates possible MYB-MYB combinatorial control points in phenolic regulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Main-text target promoter not specified; additional details likely in supplemental data

Title: A Maize Gene Regulatory Network for Phenolic Metabolism
